mission.title: Install and Configure Clusterpedia on Kubernetes
mission.description: Clusterpedia is an encyclopedia of Kubernetes clusters that allows you to synchronize, search for, and control multi-cluster resources effectively. Installing Clusterpedia enables you to manage your Kubernetes resources across multiple clusters seamlessly.

steps.title: Add the Clusterpedia Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Clusterpedia Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration using the following command:
```bash
helm repo add clusterpedia https://clusterpedia-io.github.io/clusterpedia
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Clusterpedia using Helm
steps.description:
Install Clusterpedia in a new namespace called 'clusterpedia-system' with the specified version:
```bash
helm install clusterpedia clusterpedia/clusterpedia --version v0.9.0 --namespace clusterpedia-system --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Clusterpedia pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n clusterpedia-system
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for Clusterpedia
steps.description:
Edit the Clusterpedia deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to open the deployment configuration:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment clusterpedia -n clusterpedia-system
```
Then add the following under the `spec.template.spec.containers.resources` section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"
```

steps.title: Expose the Clusterpedia API server
steps.description:
To access the Clusterpedia API server, set up port forwarding:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/clusterpedia-apiserver -n clusterpedia-system 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Clusterpedia will result in the Clusterpedia pods running in the 'clusterpedia-system' namespace. You can access the API server locally via port 8080.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add clusterpedia https://clusterpedia-io.github.io/clusterpedia
helm repo update
helm install clusterpedia clusterpedia/clusterpedia --version v0.9.0 --namespace clusterpedia-system --create-namespace

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Clusterpedia release from the Kubernetes cluster using Helm to remove all associated resources except for CRDs and persistent volumes.
```bash
helm uninstall clusterpedia --namespace clusterpedia-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) created by Clusterpedia and any persistent volume claims (PVCs) if they are no longer needed.
```bash
kubectl delete crd clusterpediaresources.clusterpedia.io
kubectl delete crd clusterpedias.clusterpedia.io
kubectl delete pvc --all -n clusterpedia-system
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'clusterpedia-system' namespace and verify that it has been removed along with all its resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace clusterpedia-system
kubectl get namespaces | grep clusterpedia-system
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of the Clusterpedia installation by exporting the existing resources.
```bash
kubectl get all -n clusterpedia-system -o yaml > clusterpedia-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the Clusterpedia release to the latest version. Ensure to specify the desired version.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade clusterpedia clusterpedia/clusterpedia --version v0.10.0 --namespace clusterpedia-system
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the Clusterpedia pods are running the new version successfully after the upgrade.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n clusterpedia-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Clusterpedia pods are stuck in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for the specific pod to diagnose the issue.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n clusterpedia-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the Clusterpedia API server service is not reachable, ensure that the service is running and check the port forwarding command.
```bash
kubectl get svc -n clusterpedia-system
kubectl port-forward svc/clusterpedia-apiserver -n clusterpedia-system 8080:80
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources allocated
troubleshooting.description:
If the pods are not starting due to insufficient resources, verify the resource limits and requests set in the deployment.
```bash
kubectl describe deployment clusterpedia -n clusterpedia-system
```

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter errors related to missing CRDs, ensure that the CRDs were not deleted accidentally. You can recreate them if necessary.
```bash
kubectl get crd
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed.
